name: Grilled Eggplant Pita Pizzas
method: Split the ¾-inch pieces of eggplant.
Position it over a sheet in a saucepan; season with salt and flip.
Enable thirty minutes to stand.
In the meantime, in a cup, toss 1 tablespoon of oil with red pepper,
garlic, and onion.
Shift to a griddle or clear grill basket for grilling; put on the grill rack.
Barbecue, exposed, 8-12 minutes over a moderate flame or until
veggies are finely charred and crunchy, stirring regularly.
Rinse the eggplants and drain them; dry them with towels.
Rub one tablespoon of oil with the eggplants; spray with pepper.
Grill, sealed, 4-5 minutes on either side until it is soft, over a
moderate fire.
Break into quarters for each piece.
Brush the pita bread on both edges with the remaining butter.
Grill, sealed, for two minutes over medium-high heat or until
the bottoms are finely browned. Retract from the fire.
Layer the grilled pitas with grilled peppers, onions, olives, and
cheese.
Sprinkle with pepper seasoning if necessary.
Return to the grill; cook three minutes, covered, or until the
cheese is melted.
Toss basil with that too.
Chapter 5: Vegetarian Greek Recipes
